sections.name: Class Management

assertions.rule: Always use cn() helper (twMerge + clsx) for conditional/merged classes
assertions.check: Response uses cn() (or mentions twMerge + clsx combined helper) instead of template literal concatenation

assertions.rule: Use cva() for reusable component variants, not @apply
assertions.check: Response recommends cva() for variant definitions

assertions.rule: Avoid @apply except for global base/typography prose styles
assertions.check: Response explicitly discourages @apply for component variants, limiting it to base/prose styles

assertions.rule: Class order: layout > sizing > spacing > typography > visual > interactive > dark > responsive
assertions.check: Response specifies the prescribed class ordering convention

assertions.rule: Use tailwind-merge to resolve conflicting utility classes
assertions.check: Response mentions tailwind-merge for conflict resolution

sections.name: Responsive & Mobile-First
sections.prompt:
I'm building a card grid layout. I want it to be 1 column on desktop and 3 columns on mobile. Here's my code: ```tsx
``` Also, I have a heading that should be large everywhere: ```tsx

Welcome

``` How should I fix these?

assertions.rule: Always design mobile-first: base = mobile, add sm:/md:/lg: for larger
assertions.check: Response corrects to mobile-first approach: base styles for mobile, breakpoint prefixes (sm:/md:/lg:) for larger screens

assertions.rule: Responsive text: text-4xl sm:text-5xl lg:text-6xl, never fixed large sizes
assertions.check: Response shows responsive text scaling pattern instead of fixed text-6xl

assertions.rule: Container queries: @container parent + @lg:/@2xl: children for component-level responsiveness
assertions.check: Response mentions container queries as an option for component-level responsiveness

sections.name: Spacing & Sizing
sections.prompt:
Review this component's spacing and sizing: ```tsx

Hello

``` Are there any issues with the spacing values or sizing approach?

assertions.rule: Use spacing scale increments of 4 (4, 6, 8, 12, 16, 24), avoid odd values like p-5 mb-7
assertions.check: Response flags p-5 and mb-7 as non-standard spacing values and recommends multiples of 4

assertions.rule: Use Tailwind size classes (text-base) not arbitrary px values (text-[16px])
assertions.check: Response flags text-[16px] and recommends text-base or standard Tailwind size class

assertions.rule: Use size-* shorthand instead of w-* h-* when both dimensions are equal
assertions.check: Response recommends size-12 instead of w-12 h-12, and size-8 instead of w-8 h-8

sections.name: Semantic Tokens & Colors

assertions.rule: Prefer semantic tokens (bg-primary, bg-card, text-foreground) over raw colors (bg-white, bg-blue-500)
assertions.check: Response recommends semantic token names like bg-primary, text-foreground instead of raw color utilities

assertions.rule: CSS variable-based theming with rgb() for opacity support
assertions.check: Response shows CSS variable-based theming pattern

assertions.rule: Define colors in @theme { --color-*: value } with OKLCH format
assertions.check: Response uses or mentions OKLCH color format for token definitions

assertions.rule: Use color-mix() in oklab for semi-transparent color variants
assertions.check: Response mentions color-mix() for creating semi-transparent variants

assertions.rule: Use --color-*: initial to clear defaults before custom palette
assertions.check: Response mentions using initial to clear default colors

assertions.rule: Define colors in @theme { --color-*: value }
assertions.check: Response uses @theme block for color token definitions

sections.name: Accessibility
sections.prompt:
I have these interactive elements: ```tsx Settings
``` Are there any accessibility concerns?

assertions.rule: Focus styles required on all interactive elements (focus:ring-2 focus:ring-offset-2)
assertions.check: Response adds focus:ring-2 focus:ring-offset-2 or similar focus ring styles to interactive elements

assertions.rule: Minimum 44x44px touch targets on mobile interactive elements
assertions.check: Response flags the small p-1 button as too small for touch and recommends minimum 44x44px

assertions.rule: Use sr-only for screen-reader-only text; icon buttons need aria-label
assertions.check: Response adds aria-label to icon button or uses sr-only span for accessible text

assertions.rule: Respect motion-reduce: add motion-reduce:transition-none for animations
assertions.check: Response adds motion-reduce variant to the spinning animation

assertions.rule: Use contrast-more: variant for high-contrast mode support
assertions.check: Response mentions contrast-more: variant for high-contrast support

sections.name: Animations & Performance
sections.prompt:
I'm adding animations to my component: ```tsx
``` Also, I want to animate a popover entrance. What's the best approach?

assertions.rule: Only animate GPU-accelerated properties (transform, opacity); avoid transition-all with w/h
assertions.check: Response flags transition-all with width/height as problematic and recommends transform/opacity

assertions.rule: Skeleton loading: animate-pulse + rounded-md bg-gray-200 dark:bg-gray-800
assertions.check: Response shows the animate-pulse skeleton pattern with proper dark mode colors

assertions.rule: Use @starting-style for native popover/entry animations
assertions.check: Response mentions @starting-style for popover/entry animations

assertions.rule: Define animation tokens as --animate-* and @keyframes inside @theme blocks
assertions.check: Response defines animation tokens in @theme blocks

sections.name: Dark Mode
sections.prompt:
I'm implementing dark mode in my Tailwind v4 project. I have some v3 code:

```js
// tailwind.config.js
module.exports = {
  darkMode: 'class',
  // ...
}
```

```tsx
<html className="dark">
  <body className="bg-white text-black dark:bg-gray-900 dark:text-white">
    <div className="border border-gray-200 dark:border-gray-700">
```

How should I migrate dark mode to v4?

assertions.rule: Dark mode v4: built-in, use @custom-variant dark (&:where(.dark, .dark *))
assertions.check: Response shows @custom-variant dark with the :where(.dark, .dark *) selector for v4

assertions.rule: Override dark colors via .dark { --color-*: value } in CSS
assertions.check: Response shows overriding color variables in .dark {} block

assertions.rule: Set border-border on * and bg-background text-foreground on body
assertions.check: Response uses semantic tokens bg-background text-foreground on body and border-border on *

sections.name: Tailwind v4 Migration

assertions.rule: v4: @import "tailwindcss" replaces @tailwind directives
assertions.check: Response replaces @tailwind directives with @import 'tailwindcss'

assertions.rule: v4: @theme {} in CSS replaces tailwind.config.ts theme config
assertions.check: Response moves theme config to @theme {} in CSS

assertions.rule: v4: content detection is automatic, no content array needed
assertions.check: Response mentions automatic content detection and removing the content array

assertions.rule: v4: use opacity modifier syntax (bg-black/50) not bg-opacity-50
assertions.check: Response replaces bg-opacity-50 with opacity modifier syntax (bg-brand/50)

assertions.rule: Replace tailwindcss-animate plugin with native CSS animations in v4
assertions.check: Response mentions replacing tailwindcss-animate with native CSS animations

assertions.rule: v4: @utility directive for custom utilities with variant support
assertions.check: Response mentions @utility directive for custom utilities

assertions.rule: v4: @custom-variant for custom conditional styles
assertions.check: Response mentions @custom-variant directive

assertions.rule: v4: use @tailwindcss/postcss or @tailwindcss/vite for build integration
assertions.check: Response mentions @tailwindcss/postcss or @tailwindcss/vite

assertions.rule: Scope content paths tightly; never include node_modules or overly broad globs
assertions.check: Response flags node_modules in content paths as problematic

sections.name: Layout Patterns
sections.prompt:
I have these layout issues: ```tsx {/* Grid with long text */}

This is a very long text that might overflow the grid cell and cause layout issues in production

{/* Image card */}

Caption

{/* Stacking layers */}
``` What improvements should I make?

assertions.rule: Add min-w-0 + truncate in grid cells to prevent text overflow
assertions.check: Response adds min-w-0 and truncate (or line-clamp) to grid cells

assertions.rule: Use aspect-video/aspect-square with object-cover to prevent layout shift on images
assertions.check: Response adds aspect-ratio and object-cover to the image

assertions.rule: Z-index: systematic increments of 10 (0, 10, 20, 40, 50)
assertions.check: Response flags arbitrary z-index values and recommends systematic increments of 10

assertions.rule: Truncation: truncate for single line, line-clamp-N for multi-line
assertions.check: Response mentions truncate and/or line-clamp for text overflow handling

sections.name: Components & React Integration

assertions.rule: Use compound component pattern for complex UI (Card, Dialog)
assertions.check: Response recommends compound component pattern (Dialog.Trigger, Dialog.Content, etc.)

assertions.rule: Use Radix primitives for accessible unstyled components
assertions.check: Response recommends Radix (or similar headless) primitives for the Dialog

assertions.rule: React 19: ref is a regular prop, no forwardRef needed
assertions.check: Response flags forwardRef as unnecessary in React 19

assertions.rule: Validate forms with Zod + React Hook Form
assertions.check: Response recommends Zod + React Hook Form for form validation

assertions.rule: Never concatenate dynamic classes (text-${color}-500); use full string maps or safelist
assertions.check: Response warns against dynamic class concatenation
